steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to identify what causes meanders. The notes state the mechanism directly: water erodes sediment from one side of the riverbank and redeposits it on the opposite side. The correct choice must state this cause-and-effect relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Over time, many meanders have formed in the Araguaia River, a river in Brazil with high sinuosity.': this describes a property of the Araguaia River but says nothing about what causes meanders to form. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The meanders in the Araguaia River will change shape and shift downstream over time.': this describes what meanders do after they form, not what causes them. ✗
  • Choice C — 'High sinuosity is caused by the formation of many meanders over time.': this reverses the relationship — sinuosity describes a river with many meanders, but the notes say nothing about sinuosity causing meanders. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The erosion and redepositing of sediment from one side of a riverbank to the other forms meanders over time.': this directly states the causal mechanism given in the notes. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
